text: Computer vision and deep learning for food recognition (food classification, calorie estimation, quality inspection) have achieved >90% accuracy on benchmark datasets, enabling AI-powered dietary tracking and food safety monitoring.
FAO reports that AI can optimize food supply chains through predictive analytics, reducing post-harvest losses by 20-30% and improving food security for vulnerable populations.

NotCo (Chilean food-tech unicorn, $1.5B valuation) uses the "Giuseppe" AI platform to discover plant-based alternatives to animal products by analyzing molecular structures of foods and matching them with plant ingredients.
